summary: Test code is well-structured and designed to cover all major requirements (version parsing, bump detection, file updates, changelog generation, workflow validation). Unit tests comprehensively cover individual functions; act integration tests verify CI/CD pipeline behavior. However, test suite is NON-FUNCTIONAL: critical infrastructure files are referenced but not provided, preventing test execution. Missing files: SemanticVersionBumper.psm1 (imported line 1 of SemanticVersionBumper.Tests.ps1), .github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml (tested by Workflow.Tests.ps1), tests/fixtures/commits-{minor,major,patch,none}.txt (used in act tests), .actrc (copied during act setup). Additionally, rigor gaps: no tests for empty inputs, pre-release/build-metadata versions, special characters in commits, or changelog append behavior; act test suite only covers 3 scenarios, omitting the 'none' case tested in units. Would not trust this suite to catch regressions in its current incomplete state.
